headline: The Week In Numbers: SK Hynix shares plunge, mass Volkswagen layoff fears
summary:
STORY: From a huge hit for SK Hynix shares to mass layoffs at Volkswagen.

:: The Week In Numbers

This is The Week In Numbers.

:: 15%

15% is the fall SK Hynix shares took in trading on Monday in the South Korean chip giants biggest one-day decline on record.

It came just days after it's U.S. listing as investors in Seoul cashed out of a massive share price rally following its Nasdaq debut last week.

Monday's stock drop kicked off a volatile week for the company's shares.

:: 25%

25% is the plunge IBM shares took after it signaled the AI infrastructure boom is drawing spending away from the software sector.

Big Blue said it expected revenue to rise just 1% in the second quarter, falling short of Wall Street hopes. 

In a letter to investors, CEO Arvind Krishna said the firm had "faltered" in keeping pace with a shift in corporate spending from software to data-center infrastructure.

:: 11%

11% is the hit global smartphone shipments took in the second quarter falling to their lowest level for the period since 2013.

That's according to early estimates from Counterpoint Research, which said a memory chip shortage has driven up smartphone prices and dampened demand.

Memory chip prices have soared as suppliers prioritize AI data center customers over consumer electronics.

:: 50,000

50,000 jobs may need to be cut at Volkswagen to match the competitiveness of rivals.

That's what its CEO told staff in an internal memo.

It effectively confirms for the first time that the automaker is looking to reduce up to 100,000 positions.

Sources also told Reuters last month VW could close four German factories. 

:: $14.8 billion

$14.8 billion is the equity value Uber placed on German firm Delivery Hero, as it launched a takeover bid of the food-delivery company.

Were a deal to go ahead it would create the largest food-delivery group outside China.

An acquisition would further the U.S. ride-hailing firm's efforts to build a global food-delivery business, as competition with U.S. and European rivals heats up.
